section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is concerned about potentially contracting an STD and reports infidelity.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This restates the patient's reported concern and history (partner infidelity), which are subjective elements rather than clinician assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is high risk due to an abnormal pap smear in the past and the recent discovery of her sexual partner having multiple partners.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a risk stratification statement based on past objective result (abnormal Pap) and documented history; it is more an interpretation of existing findings than a diagnostic impression or problem list.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will be tested for gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomoniasis, syphilis, HIV and hepatitis c.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an explicit management action (ordering tests) and belongs in the plan, not in the assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will be tested for Hepatitis C. The patient will be tested for gonorrhea. The patient will be tested for chlamydia. The patient will be tested for trichomoniasis. The patient will be tested for syphilis. The patient will be tested for HIV. The patient will be tested for hepatitis c.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Content is appropriate for the Plan, but it is redundant with the testing statement already (mis)placed in the Assessment; ideally, all test-order statements would be consolidated in P and removed from A.
